children.heading: Determined to be obscene
children.label: 1 Determined to be obscene
children.text: The term “determined to be obscene” means determined, in a final judgment of a court of record and of competent jurisdiction in the United States, to be obscene.

children.label: A
children.text: using technology to enable users to find, evaluate, organize, create, and communicate information; and

children.label: B
children.text: developing digital citizenship and the responsible use of technology.

children.heading: Director
children.label: 3 Director
children.text: The term “Director” means the Director of the Institute appointed under .section 9103 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: not reviewed by any other court that has authority to review such judgment; or

children.label: B
children.text: not reviewable by any other court.

children.heading: Indian tribe
children.label: 5 Indian tribe
children.text: The term “Indian tribe” means any tribe, band, nation, or other organized group or community, including any Alaska native village, regional corporation, or village corporation (as defined in, or established pursuant to, the Alaska Native Claims Settlement Act ( et seq.)), which is recognized as eligible for the special programs and services provided by the United States to Indians because of their status as Indians.43 U.S.C. 1601

children.heading: Institute
children.label: 6 Institute
children.text: The term “Institute” means the Institute of Museum and Library Services established under .section 9102 of this title

children.heading: Museum and Library Services Board
children.label: 7 Museum and Library Services Board
children.text: The term “Museum and Library Services Board” means the National Museum and Library Services Board established under .section 9105a of this title

children.label: A
children.text: the average person, applying contemporary community standards, would find that such project, when taken as a whole, appeals to the prurient interest;

children.label: B
children.text: such project depicts or describes sexual conduct in a patently offensive way; and

children.label: C
children.text: such project, when taken as a whole, lacks serious literary, artistic, political, or scientific value.
